D3 Chemistry L5 Evaluate Reference

Benzene → Naphthalene → Anthracene → Tetracene

linearly fusing aromatic rings extends the π system one ring at a time; each fusion red-shifts the lowest UV-Vis absorption by ~50–100 nm

Benzene, naphthalene, anthracene, tetracene are the first four members of the linear-acene series. Each added ring extends the conjugated π system, lowers the LUMO, and red-shifts the lowest absorption (the α band). At tetracene the absorption falls firmly into the visible — the crystal is orange. Pentacene (5 rings) is deep blue.

Toggle the number of fused rings. The structure on the left grows; the absorption marker on the spectrum on the right slides into the red; the swatch shows the resulting (complementary) crystal colour.
